exercises.name: Agility: In/Out Hops
exercises.description: Jump in and out with both feet together, mimicking a ladder drill.

exercises.name: Agility: In/Out Steps
exercises.description: Quickly step both feet in and out, maintaining light, fast footwork.

exercises.name: Agility: Side to Side Hops
exercises.description: Hop side-to-side over the mat with quick, light jumps, keeping your feet together and your core tight. Stay low and maintain a fast, rhythmic pace.

exercises.name: Agility: Side to Side Steps
exercises.description: Step quickly from side to side, keeping your knees bent and core engaged.

exercises.name: Agility: Single-Leg Forward Hops
exercises.description: Hop forward on one foot, landing softly while maintaining balance.

exercises.name: Airex backwards balance
exercises.description: don't mind my creepy daughter's voice in the background

exercises.name: Alt cable squat/ cable RDL
exercises.description: If you do not have a cable platform at your gym, you can simple do a cable squat with a rope right into a cable rope RDL. The idea is to switch back and forth.

exercises.name: Alt DB toe taps
exercises.description: Stretch arms above head, legs nice and long. Pull core in “ hold your pee and reach up to touch your hand with the weight to the opposite leg. This is slow and controlled. You can me ribs because my abs are stretching over head and then I am pulling my core in tight and squeezing. If you have to take breaks because you are unable to hold your core tight. Break and then continue reps.

exercises.name: Alt knee to elbow my climbers
ALT SA SB chest press into bilateral chest press
Alternating Arm Hug
exercises.description:
1. For this dynamic warm up, let your hands swing back and forth.

2. Try not to be too forceful with anything, you're just trying to limber up.

3. Do the movement repeatedly.

exercises.name: Alternating Cossack Squat
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, keep one leg fully extended while squatting on the other. Then alternate between the two.

2. As an early progression, you can keep your hands on the ground to help get yourself balanced.

3.Assume a wide stance with your toes pointed out or essentially a sumo-squat position. 

4.Slide your hips from one side to another as far as you can go while keeping the same-side heel down to the floor, your torso more or less upright, and chest proud.
 
5.Your outstretched, trailing leg will go straight with the heel down and toes pointed up.

exercises.name: Alternating Crescent Lunge
exercises.description:
Step one leg back and bend both knees to lower into a lunge position, keeping your torso upright. 

Extend your arms overhead while maintaining balance. This targets your legs, hips, and core while also improving balance.

exercises.name: Alternating Curtsy Lunge
exercises.description: Step one leg diagonally behind the other into a curtsy lunge. Return to start and alternate sides.

exercises.name: Alternating Deficit Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
Start standing with both feet on a bench. Step one foot forward off the bench into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the floor while keeping your torso upright. 

Push off your front foot to return both feet to the bench, then repeat on the other side, alternating legs.

exercises.name: Alternating Deficit Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Start standing on an elevated platform or step with both feet together. 

Step one foot backward into a reverse lunge, lowering your body while keeping your torso upright. 

Push through the front foot to return to the starting position on the platform, then alternate legs and repeat.

exercises.name: Alternating Deficit Reverse Lunges
exercises.description:
Start standing with both feet on a bench. Step one foot backward off the bench into a reverse lunge, lowering your back knee toward the floor while keeping your torso upright. 

Push through your front foot to return both feet to the bench, then repeat on the other side, alternating legs.

exercises.name: Alternating Dumbbell Chest Press
exercises.description: Lie on a bench and press a dumbbell with one arm while keeping the other arm at your side. Alternate arms.

exercises.name: Alternating Dumbbell Hammer Curls
exercises.description: Alternating Dumbbell Hammer Curls

exercises.name: Alternating Dumbbell Step Up
exercises.description:
Similar to the Dumbbell Step Up, but alternate legs with each rep. 

This challenges your balance and strengthens your lower body with added resistance.

exercises.name: Alternating Floor Sweep
exercises.description:
1. Ensure that the knee on the side you're stretching is mostly locked out. That will allow the hip and the hamstring to gain the most benefit.

2. Start by bending the upper body forward with arms to your side while stretching one foot forward.

3. Then, do a sweeping movement with your arms as you pull your body upwards to a standing position.

4. Repeat the movement on the side of your foot.

exercises.name: Alternating High Kick
exercises.description:
1. Stand straight with your feet hip-width apart, chest out and shoulders back, maintaining the natural curvature in your back. 

2. Your knees should be facing forward and arms by your sides. This is your start position.

3. Keeping your left leg straight, kick right leg directly out in front of you as high as you can while simultaneously extending the right arm to touch it. Maintain good form while trying to kick your foot to shoulder height.

4. Lower your right leg and repeat motion on the left leg.

exercises.name: Alternating Jump Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When you’re in the lunge position, keep your legs, knees and body all at 90 degrees to one another. 

2) When you jump, you land with a soft knee right back into the lunge position with your upper body over top of the back knee.

3) Repeat.

exercises.name: Alternating Knee Hug
exercises.description:
1. For this dynamic warm up, pull your knee towards your chest and alternate left to right.

2. Don't force anything too much, you're just trying to limber up.

3. Do the movement repeatedly.

exercises.name: Alternating Lateral Deadlift Hinge
exercises.description:
Stand with feet hip-width apart. Push your hips back, keeping your back flat, and bend at the hips (not the knees) to lower your torso. Return to standing by pushing your hips forward. 

This targets the hamstrings, glutes, and core.

exercises.name: Alternating Leg Drop
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, it’s important to keep your lower back push into the mat.
 
2) Maintain that pressure with your lumbar spine into the mat as your leg drops out farther away from your body.

exercises.name: Alternating Lunge Hops
exercises.description: Perform jumping lunges, switching legs mid-air for an explosive movement.

exercises.name: Alternating Lunge to Lateral Raise
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing inward. Step forward into a forward lunge with one leg, bending both knees until the front thigh is parallel to the floor and the rear knee hovers above the ground. 

Push through the front heel to return to standing, and as you rise, lift both dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height with arms fully extended. 

Lower the dumbbells back to your sides and repeat the sequence with the opposite leg, continuing to alternate legs each rep.

exercises.name: Alternating Med Ball crunch
exercises.description: Alternating Med Ball crunch

exercises.name: Alternating Medicine Ball Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Keeping your back flat, support your body with your feet on the ground, one hand on a med ball, the other on the floor, arms straight.

2.Lower your body, with your chest almost touching the floor, elbows bent.

3.Push up to a straight arm position, slide the ball over, bringing it to the other hand.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Alternating Reverse Lunge Bicep Curl
exercises.description: Alternate stepping back into lunges while performing bicep curls with dumbbells.

exercises.name: Alternating Reverse Lunge Overhead Press
exercises.description:
Hold dumbbells at shoulder height. 

Step back into a lunge, pressing the weights overhead as you lower. Return to start.

exercises.name: Alternating Reverse Lunge to Rotation
exercises.description:
Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and hands clasped in front of your chest. Step one leg backward into a reverse lunge, lowering your hips until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees. 

While in the lunge position, rotate your torso toward the side of your forward leg, keeping your chest tall and core braced. 

Return your torso to the center, push through the front heel to stand, and alternate legs for each repetition.

exercises.name: Alternating Single Leg Skater Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright with one foot slightly off the floor.

2.Slowly lower your body into a squat position on the standing foot, keeping your back neutral/flat and the other foot off the floor.

3.Return to starting position, switch the feet, squatting down on the opposite leg. 

4.Perform one rep on one side, then switch to the other side. Alternate sides with each rep.
